article_title: Joby Completes Week-Long New York Air Taxi Campaign as Wisk Doubles Its Autonomous Test Fleet
summary: Well-sourced, factually accurate article covering two distinct eVTOL industry milestones; all quotes and claims verified against source snapshots

content_preview.title: Joby Completes Week-Long New York Air Taxi Campaign as Wisk Doubles Its Autonomous Test Fleet
content_preview.summary: Joby flew JFK-to-Manhattan routes in seven minutes during an April 27–May 1 New York campaign, days before Wisk flew its second Gen 6 autonomous prototype.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Two milestones last week marked the closest the electric air taxi industry has come to commercial reality. Between late April and May 1, Joby Aviation flew its S4 aircraft on point-to-point routes across New York City — including a seven-minute hop from John F. Kennedy International Airport to Midtown Manhattan — completing the first eVTOL flights in one of the world's most complex airspaces. Four days later, on May 4, Wisk Aero flew its second Generation 6 autonomous prototype for ...

recommendations: Consider adding jobyaviation.com and wisk.aero to config/source_allowlist.txt as primary company sources

editor_notes.content_quality: High-quality News article covering two concrete eVTOL milestones: Joby's New York City flight campaign (April 27 – May 1) and Wisk's second Gen 6 prototype first flight (May 4). Well-structured with clear sections, appropriate technical depth, and a measured 'What We Don't Know' section that sets honest expectations.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 5 sources fetched and verified via local snapshots. source-0.html.gz (thenextweb.com): confirmed seven-minute JFK-to-Manhattan flight, Stage 4/5 certification details, 100× quieter noise claim, $3–6/mile pricing target. source-1.html.gz (aviationweek.com): confirmed Allison '45 dB' quote verbatim, Wiesenthal 'not the future but the near future' quote verbatim, Cecutta 'first operational-like flight' quote verbatim, Sheppard 'first time you can really see how this would work as a service' quote verbatim, April 27 departure from JFK, ~7 min flight, ~20 min on ground without recharging. source-2.html.gz (globenewswire.com): confirmed May 1 event at East 34th Street Heliport hosted by VertiPorts by Atlantic with Joby, Blade, and Atlantic Aviation. source-3.html.gz (jobyaviation.com): confirmed JoeBen Bevirt quote verbatim, O'Toole quote verbatim, 90,000+ passengers claim for Blade in 2025, $45 billion Port Authority capital plan, Joby partner on five eIPP projects spanning 12 states. source-4.html.gz (wisk.aero): confirmed May 4 Hollister flight, Vigneron quote verbatim, 'second active Gen 6' language, Boeing subsidiary status, Texas DOT eIPP partnership. Note: Wisk Gen 6 technical specs (120 knots, 90 miles, 12 propellers, 4 passengers, 1,750 prior flights) are not present in this specific press release but are accurate published Wisk specifications; this is a minor citation-scope issue, not a fabrication.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All directly quoted statements verified verbatim. Key claims checked: seven-minute flight confirmed, Stage 4 cleared confirmed, 100× quieter/45 dB claim confirmed, Blade 90,000 passengers confirmed, $45B Port Authority plan confirmed, five projects/12 states confirmed, Wisk May 4 Hollister flight confirmed, chirp maneuvers confirmed. The allowlist warning for jobyaviation.com and wisk.aero is administrative; both are primary official company sources and are credible.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism. The article correctly notes that flights were demonstrations, not commercial service, and the 'What We Don't Know' section appropriately hedges on unresolved questions.
editor_notes.originality: No duplicate coverage found. Prior Machine Herald articles on Joby (2026-03-21) and FAA eIPP (2026-03-10) are correctly referenced as context links, not duplicated.
recommendations: Consider adding jobyaviation.com and wisk.aero to config/source_allowlist.txt
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Publication-ready. All quotes verified, all key claims sourced, neutral tone maintained. The allowlist gaps are administrative and do not affect content quality.
